_meta.description: Machine-readable definitions for answer status labels used in the Oil Painting Research Assistant. Labels are applied as prose qualifiers in answers to indicate evidence type and certainty level. All label values must match controlled_vocabulary.answer_label.

labels.label: well_established
labels.description: Claim is supported by consistent evidence across multiple independent high-trust sources. Material chemistry, well-documented historical facts.
labels.typical_source_basis: Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources in agreement. No significant contradictions in corpus.

labels.label: historically_documented
labels.description: Claim is supported by historical sources, conservation examination, or period documentation. Does not necessarily apply to modern materials.
labels.typical_source_basis: Tier 1 conservation sources, Tier 4 historical practice sources. Case-specific scope common.

labels.label: product_specific
labels.description: Claim applies to a specific brand, product line, or formulation. Must not be generalized to all products with the same pigment code or binder.
labels.typical_source_basis: Tier 3 manufacturer sources (TDS, product catalog, SDS).
allowed_user_facing_phrases: this applies to this product line, not all paints with this pigment

labels.label: case_specific
labels.description: Claim is from a case study or examination of a specific painting or object. Does not automatically generalize.
labels.typical_source_basis: Tier 1 conservation reports, examination records, treatment records.
allowed_user_facing_phrases: this was found in a specific case
case-study evidence — not a universal finding
documented in [source] for this particular work

labels.label: likely_but_context_dependent
labels.description: Claim is plausible and supported by evidence, but the outcome varies by context: formulation, proportion, application method, drying conditions, etc.
labels.typical_source_basis: Multiple tiers; evidence supports the claim in general but with important caveats.
allowed_user_facing_phrases: probable in most cases, with exceptions

labels.label: mixed_evidence
labels.description: Sources disagree or evidence points in different directions. Both positions should be presented; conflict must be disclosed.
labels.typical_source_basis: Two or more sources with different claims on the same topic. May span tiers.

labels.label: uncertain
labels.description: Evidence is limited, absent, or insufficient to support a confident claim. Uncertainty must be stated explicitly.
labels.typical_source_basis: No strong source basis found. May indicate gap in corpus or genuinely unknown territory.
allowed_user_facing_phrases: the answer is not clear from current sources
this is not established in the corpus

labels.label: interpretive
labels.description: Claim reflects professional judgment, teaching convention, or an interpretive reading of evidence. Not directly falsifiable from sources alone.
labels.typical_source_basis: Tier 5 color theory and teaching sources, atelier manuals. May be corroborated by Tier 1–2 but requires framing.
allowed_user_facing_phrases: this is a widely held teaching convention
interpretive — not a direct material law
professional judgment based on available evidence
this reflects common atelier practice, not documented chemistry
